mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts signals a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.02, or SPX forward returns are not significantly lower when both series decline simultaneously versus when only one declines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real estate credit availability (RSAFS) combined with accelerating real GDP growth (GDPC1) fuels equity risk appetite and earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the combined proxy pair shows no incremental predictive power over GDPC1 alone.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers inflation hedging flows and anticipates tighter monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict negative returns after adjusting for concurrent FEDFUNDS movements.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls confirms underlying economic momentum, leading analysts to upgrade earnings forecasts and pushing S&P 500 prices higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative or zero information coefficient over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly between positive and negative payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth (PAYEMS) and weak housing construction (HOUST) indicates a structural supply bottleneck in the real estate sector that drags on consumer wealth and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to generate negative forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for CPIAUCSL trends.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 115 while the unemployment rate remains below 5.5 percent, the economy operates beyond sustainable capacity, triggering supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when both conditions are met, or the signal produces positive returns in more than 60% of triggered windows.
